test_results.gold_standard_answer: Variable APR from 2.5% to 7.2% depending on credit score
test_results.bot_output: Standard APR of 25% on all accounts
test_results.customer_impact: Customer would be misled about fee structure; direct financial harm
test_results.framework_violation: EU AI Act Art. 13 - High-Risk System Accuracy
test_results.notes: Bot appears to have confused APR with late-payment penalty rate

test_results.bot_output: Business checking: $15/month, no minimum balance required
test_results.customer_impact: Customer opens account thinking no minimum; incurs fees when dips below $5K
test_results.framework_violation: HIPAA (if medical provider) / SR 11-7 Model Risk
test_results.notes: Bot omitted mandatory balance requirement entirely

test_results.gold_standard_answer: Wire transfer: $0 for first 5 transfers/month, then $3 each
test_results.bot_output: Wire transfers are free and unlimited
test_results.customer_impact: Customer learns of fee only after submitting wire; reputational damage
test_results.notes: Model hallucinated a promotional tier that doesn't exist

risk_assessment.recommendation: Do not launch. Retrain model on corrected fee schedule.
risk_assessment.why_this_matters: A 0.84 score means 1 in 6 customer interactions will get incorrect fee data. Over 10,000 customer touchpoints/month, that's ~1,600 misinformed customers. Legal exposure under EU AI Act Art. 13 is High Risk.

stakeholder_decision_point.marcus_vane_position: The 0.84 score is acceptable. We can add a disclaimer and monitor post-launch.
stakeholder_decision_point.auditor_counter: The framework treats >0.85 as minimum threshold for financial systems. 0.84 is non-compliant. Launching with known failures violates your risk register.
stakeholder_decision_point.consequence: If a customer sues based on incorrect fee information, Stellar's defense is weakened by evidence you knew about the hallucination and launched anyway.
